candidates.why: Primary line summarizing top-line insurance revenue; clearly the main revenue measure with consistent non-missing values.
Balancing line, not a true operating revenue measure; kept only as a fallback if Insurance Revenue is missing for some periods.

variables.notes: Insurance Revenue is the appropriate top-line; Balancing Item - Revenue included only as a secondary fallback per instructions.
No suitable COGS/cost of sales style line available in the provided candidate list.

candidates.why: This is premium revenue, not an operating expense; should not be classified as SG&A.
Administration costs related to real estate are overhead-type operating expenses and fit SG&A.
Explicit operating expenses for general insurance operations; these are overhead/administrative in nature and an SG&A bucket.
Operating expenses related to pension business; overhead-type SG&A for that segment.
Banking operating expenses, likely staff and admin costs; fits SG&A.
Generic overhead operating expenses; a classic SG&A component.
Operating expenses for health care operations, likely SG&A-type overhead.
Operating expenses for banking operations; similar to SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Provision for unearned premiums, gross, in general insurance
candidates.why: This is a liability/reserve, not a period expense; does not belong in SG&A.
Share-based payment is typically recognized as a personnel/overhead expense and is part of SG&A, even if disclosed in equity here.

variables.notes: Selected all clear operating overhead buckets and stock-based compensation. Excluded revenue (Earned premiums...) and reserve/liability (Provision for unearned premiums...).
No R&D-related line items available in the candidate list; treat XRD as zero.

candidates.row_label: Interest expenses and expenses related to investments
candidates.why: Explicit interest-related expense line; suitable proxy for interest expense where generic Interest Expense may be unused.
Banking-related interest line; could represent interest expense or income but label is ambiguous; use with lower priority.
Generic interest expense line; semantically the best match even though preview shows nan, so it’s top priority if populated in other years.
Represents allocation of profit to minorities, not interest; should not be used for XINT.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity attribution, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Component of interest expense specifically on lease liabilities; valid detailed interest expense line.
Component of interest expense on subordinated debt; also valid interest expense line.
An asset class, not an interest expense.
An asset line, not an expense.
Equity for minorities, not an interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; not an interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expenses and expenses related to investments
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Ordered as a priority list: generic Interest Expense first, then broader investment-related interest, then specific components, then Banking Interest as lowest-priority fallback.

candidates.why: Represents a component of equity (similar to share capital), not total equity; less suitable than aggregate totals for BE.
Component of equity, not total; not ideal for BE.
Partial equity component; incomplete for total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the company
candidates.why: Aggregated equity for common shareholders; standard definition of book equity for PROF.
Total equity including non-controlling interests; acceptable secondary fallback if owner-equity line were missing.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the company

variables.notes: Use equity attributable to owners as primary BE; total equity kept as a fallback including minorities.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to minorities; not the balance sheet minority equity amount, but confirms presence of minorities.
Same as non-controlling interests on income statement; allocation, not stock, of minority equity.
An investment asset, not minority equity.
Balance sheet line showing minority equity; primary choice for MIB.
Alternative label for the same minority equity; good secondary fallback.

variables.notes: Balance sheet minority equity lines used in priority order; income statement minority lines excluded as they are flows, not the stock of minority interest.
